vocab.meaning: bước sang trang mới (nghĩa bóng), hoặc lật trang sách (nghĩa đen vui nhộn)
vocab.example: I prefer paper books because there's a unique satisfaction in literally turning over a new leaf as you progress through a story.

vocab.meaning: một chủ đề hoặc người nào đó hoàn toàn bí ẩn/không biết gì về nó
vocab.example: To me, E-readers were a closed book until recently, but now I find them surprisingly intuitive for daily reading.

vocab.word: take a leaf out of someone's book
vocab.ipa: /teɪk ə liːf aʊt əv ˈsʌmwʌnz bʊk/
vocab.example: I decided to take a leaf out of my brother's book and switch to a Kindle to save space in my small apartment.

vocab.meaning: thức khuya để học tập hoặc làm việc
vocab.example: When I burn the midnight oil, I prefer a screen with a warm-light filter to avoid disturbing my roommate with a bedside lamp.

vocab.meaning: mải mê/đắm chìm hoàn toàn vào một cuốn sách
vocab.example: I find it much easier to get lost in a book when it's a physical copy, as there are no digital notifications to distract me.

vocab.meaning: cảnh cáo/mắng mỏ ai đó nghiêm khắc
vocab.example: My mother used to read me the riot act for reading paperbacks under the covers with a flashlight past my bedtime.

vocab.meaning: sẵn có/nằm ngay trong tầm tay
vocab.example: The beauty of reading on a screen is having a global library at your fingertips without ever leaving your house.

vocab.word: go through something with a fine-tooth comb
vocab.ipa: /ɡəʊ θruː ˈsʌmθɪŋ wɪð ə faɪn tuːθ kəʊm/
vocab.meaning: xem xét/kiểm tra thứ gì đó cực kỳ kỹ lưỡng
vocab.example: When I need to go through a research paper with a fine-tooth comb, I always print it out on paper to annotate it better.
I find classical philosophy to be quite heavy going on a screen; it's much more digestible in a traditional book format.

vocab.meaning: dồn hết tâm trí/nhiệt huyết vào việc gì đó (thường là một cuốn sách dài)
vocab.example: There is nothing I love more than sinking my teeth into a massive hardcover fantasy novel over the weekend.
